normative_requirement: Reasoning/model processes have no ambient Android authority, privileged UID, raw service access, device/proc/sysfs access or cross-user access.
ProdX exposes no generic shell, intent, Binder transaction, URI, path, property, reflection, classloader or HAL primitive.
Provider and Android callee recheck identity, permission, AppOps, user/profile, role, policy and token constraints.
UID, PID, SID, package, user, attribution and confirmation are derived from trusted boundaries, never accepted from a model.
Registries, grants, observations, history, tokens and provider bindings are keyed by Android user/profile.
Confirmation and authentication are Android-owned trusted SystemUI/Settings operations.
Runtime never opens devices or calls HALs/kernel directly; Android framework or narrowly reviewed provider mediates.
Observation and knowledge outputs are minimized, redacted, aggregated, rate-limited, purpose-bound and retention-bounded.
Missing/dead/stale/revoked/locked/unknown conditions fail closed with typed status; no broader fallback.
High/critical and descriptor-required effects reserve durable Audit before dispatch.
Authority/Registry is a small SystemService in system_server with no model/provider/plugin/long-running execution.
Broker runs in a dedicated platform process and SELinux domain without generic execution or raw hardware access.
Built-in providers are separated by risk/authority family and do not use shared broad privilege.
AppFunction execution uses a dedicated validated adapter and never arbitrary exported components/intents.
Observation Hub is a dedicated process owning leases, minimization, redaction, backpressure and bounded queues.
Reasoning runs isolated with no Android permissions or direct Registry/provider/system-service access.
SystemUI/Settings own confirmation, auth, grant/revoke, audit/undo and visible runtime controls.
Every descriptor has one primary role and required identity, availability, schema, Android authority, security, risk, execution, audit and extension fields.
Existing checked framework manager/API is preferred; dedicated provider is used only for a missing bounded semantic operation.
Generic init/property/service/HAL/content/broadcast/component/plugin/diagnostic interfaces remain inaccessible.
Capability readiness is the conjunction of build, install, feature, owner, service, version, user, policy, dependency and health state.
ProdX never exposes LocalServices or SystemServer reflection; owning Android service must expose a narrow checked method if later needed.
Registration follows define, build/install, discover, publish, attest, activate and reconcile.
Invocation follows validate, resolve, policy, trusted UI, Audit reserve, bound authorization, provider recheck, Android execution, minimize and audit.
Observation/event records never authorize action; every action starts a new policy decision.
Knowledge operations use named fixed authority/projection/query/bounds; generic resolver/provider call is prohibited.
Extensions execute in owning processes; no plugin is loaded into system_server or Broker.
Extension/provider/model cannot self-assign lower risk, permission, trust, grant or policy authority.
Package removal, signature change, downgrade or Binder death removes/invalidates binding in the next generation.
Platform signing is not blanket trust; use dedicated signature permission/allowlist and enforce SELinux/Treble separately.
Only OS-owned policy maps facts to final low/medium/high/critical risk; providers may only raise concern.
Critical actions require fresh strong auth, explicit exact confirmation, short single-use token, no auto-retry and durable audit/recovery.
No executable provider ships before all P0 foundation and security gates complete.
Users can see, revoke, stop, audit and where possible undo ProdX actions.
P0 begins inventory/shadow only; read-only, reversible, extension execution and critical capability modes are later gated phases.
Deterministic CBOR is normative; diagnostic JSON is non-authoritative and semantically equivalent.
Core values are bounded and canonical; security-critical binary floats, executable/local handles and unbounded values are prohibited.
Trust/persistence boundary objects carry type, contract version, ID, time, issuer, schema, generation, content hash and bounded extensions.
Stable IDs use governed ProdX URNs; occurrence IDs use UUIDv7; hashes use lowercase SHA-256 form.
Namespace ownership binds to verified publisher/signing lineage; package name alone is insufficient.
Schema Profile 1 is closed, bounded, no remote/recursive/dynamic/executable references and unknown authority fields fail closed.
SemVer exact/ranges have no implicit major coercion; security-semantic changes require major review/migration.
Published definitions and occurrence records never mutate; correction/revocation/deletion/supersession is a linked new record.
CapabilityDescriptor is declarative, typed, bounded and not a handle/grant/readiness proof.
Primary role is exactly Capability, Observation, Policy, Knowledge, Event, Infrastructure or Extension provider role.
Registry-owned CapabilityState follows only declared transitions; provider self-report cannot activate itself.
Availability is context-specific, short-lived, fail-closed and never reusable as authorization.
Provider-reported health is advisory; Registry owns authoritative projection and stale evidence becomes UNKNOWN.
ProviderManifest is signed candidate metadata and cannot confer permission, risk, policy or code loading.
CapabilityProvider accepts only typed request plus valid authorization and invokes an ordinary Android-owned path.
Registry publishes atomic immutable snapshots and resolves exact binding deterministically within one generation.
ExecutionContext contains trusted derived facts, is short-lived/hash-bound and never reusable across user/transaction.
CapabilityRequest is an immutable bounded proposal, never authority; mutating requests require scoped idempotency key.
PolicyDecision binds exact request/context/descriptor/provider/schema/generation hashes and stronger/deny precedence.
ConfirmationProof is opaque, exact-transaction-bound, noncredential, short-lived and replay-resistant.
ExecutionAuthorization is least-privilege, audience/request/context/policy/proof/provider/epoch bound and nontransferable.
CapabilityResponse outcome, payload/error, provider/version/generation/audit and async/undo references obey closed invariants.
CapabilityError is typed, safe/redacted, has bounded details and stable retry disposition; no stack/secret leakage.
OperationHandle is scoped/non-authoritative; cancellation/status need current authority; undo is a new policy/audit transaction.
SubscriptionLease is bounded by principal/source/schema/purpose/budget/epochs/expiry and never grants execution.
ObservationRecord is an immutable minimized snapshot with time/freshness/quality/provenance/lease/generation/hash.
EventRecord is immutable transition evidence with source occurrence/sequence/dedupe/gap/ordering semantics.
AuditRecord is append-only/tamper-evident; effects reserve first; corrections/deletion are explicit linked records; secrets forbidden.
LearningRecord is privacy-eligible and non-authoritative; it cannot be policy, identity, permission or token.
ExtensionManifest is signed/declarative; cannot redefine core semantics, self-assign trust or load into trusted processes.
Every implementation validates exact type/schema/version/issuer/scope/generation/hash before use.
Caller, user, purpose, exact parameters, capability, provider, epochs and time bind end to end.
Revocation and stricter policy override cached readiness, decisions, authorizations and leases.
Technology-neutral positive/negative vectors cover encoding, identity, version, dependencies, state, policy, replay, errors, events, audit, privacy and extension quarantine.
Contract changes require numbered proposal, threat/privacy/compatibility/migration/test/rollback review and versioning.
Only Authority/Registry/Policy/Authorization core executes in system_server.
Broker is unique-UID dedicated domain with no platform shared UID or raw Android authority.
Observation/Event runs outside system_server and Broker in a separate failure domain.
Audit owns an independent process/domain/store and Broker cannot rewrite ledger.
Extension parsing is isolated/quarantine-only; Authority alone admits Registry state.
Learning/model workers have no execution authority and use isolated UIDs when later introduced.
SystemUI owns trusted per-transaction UI; Settings owns durable administration; neither owns policy/execution.
Provider code is partitioned by risk/authority and excluded from system_server/Broker/UI/model.
Third-party extensions remain owning app processes; AppFunctions preferred; no runtime code loading.
Android framework owner remains final software authority before native/HAL/kernel.
Android base boot succeeds with ProdX absent/disabled/quarantined/rolled back.
Use framework anchor plus dedicated runtime module; APEX cannot weaken immutable contract/placement/security.
Framework Authority lives under frameworks/base; runtime services/contract/SDK/tests under packages/modules/ProdX.
Every executable component uses proposed unique domain/UID or documented existing domain; default deny.
Only named compiled reviewed Binder/service-manager edges receive access; no enumeration for providers/models.
Registry, grants, Broker, Hub, Audit, Extension and provider state have single-writer domain-separated storage.
Authority derives identity, owns policy/grants/authorization/kill switch and contains no provider execution.
Registry authority lives in system_server; extension parsing remains out of process; cache is rebuildable.
Final deterministic policy/authorization core is platform-owned inside Authority; external output is advice only.
Broker validates/orchestrates/reconciles but cannot mint authority, inspect Registry files or execute Android managers.
Provider Framework/SDK is a no-privilege library and excludes policy, grants, discovery and generic manager adapters.
Hub source permissions stay in provider adapters; Hub owns only lease/queue/transform/delivery.
Audit is direct-boot-aware, single writer and required effects fail closed if reservation unavailable.
Extension Manager reads bounded metadata only, never loads app code and cannot mutate Registry.
Framework manager façade is non-authoritative, cannot manufacture context/cache readiness/choose endpoint/bypass UI.
Authority publishes non-ready then verifies Audit/Registry before Broker/Hub/extension/provider activation; per-user readiness is independent.
Component death follows attestation/rebind/reconcile/new-generation; stale authority invalid; unknown effects not auto-retried.
Shutdown disables authorization, drains/revokes/checkpoints; correctness does not depend on graceful callback.
Prohibited topology includes model-to-Authority/provider/service-manager, Broker-to-HAL/content, extension Registry mutation and UI/Settings-to-provider.
Every binding verifies identity/signing/contract/protocol/schema/provenance; incompatible/downgraded components quarantine.
Emergency disable stops new authorizations/leases, drains services and does not disable Android owners.
SystemServer work is bounded; no model, content query, schema compilation, event payload or blocking provider execution on critical threads.
Authority cannot depend on Broker/provider/learning implementation; contract cannot depend on Android managers; Audit cannot depend on Broker.
P0 finishes disabled-by-default with contract, identity, Authority, Registry, Policy, synthetic authorization, dedicated services, test UI/SDK/Hub/Extension and no real provider.
Only DISABLED, INVENTORY_ONLY, SHADOW_POLICY and engineering TEST_NO_OP modes exist in P0.
P0 has no real provider, model requirement, public SDK, extension execution, network/cloud or Android policy semantic change.
Each task requires prerequisites, frozen object/interface ownership, tests, security review and rollback before implementation.
Milestone done requires compile/test/security/API/dependency/provenance/rollback evidence; compile alone is insufficient.
G0–G7 gates and approvals are mandatory and cannot be bypassed by a later milestone.
P0 changes occur only in the exact directory/target ownership map and first-write milestone.
Planned targets obey compile dependency graph and prohibited dependencies; compile arrows do not grant runtime authority.
Every IPC freezes caller/callee identity, objects, bounds, threading, timeout, death, user, version, security, errors and fuzz corpus before implementation.
P0 startup orders Android prerequisites, non-ready Authority, verified Audit, Registry generation, Broker/Hub/UI/Extension and test provider only.
P0-00 records hashes, threat-to-test requirements, ownership, prohibited primitives and pristine behavior baseline.
P0-01 adds skeleton/test/provenance only and does not change boot/API behavior.
P0-02 covers every contract object/field/enum/bound/state with independent positive/negative/differential vectors.
P0-03 is system/private only, fails safely when absent/dead and exposes no generic/public API.
P0-04 starts one feature-gated non-ready Authority and remains disabled/inventory until dependencies/gates.
P0-05 publishes only verified infrastructure/synthetic descriptors and deterministic generations; dynamic cache is non-authoritative.
P0-06 decisions/tokens are synthetic only; missing facts deny and dispatch remains impossible.
P0-07 uses independent single-writer journal, DE/CE partitioning, crash points and forbidden-payload rejection.
P0-08 only produces synthetic trusted proofs/admin state and remains safe if UI/runtime absent/dead.
P0-09 production dispatch is hard-disabled; TEST_NO_OP requires engineering build, exact test namespace/signature/mode.
P0-10 SDK has no policy/discovery/manager authority; no-op provider has zero capability permission and production absence.
P0-11 accepts synthetic sources only; queues/leases/revocation/gaps are bounded and metadata audit excludes payload.
P0-12 accepts bounded sealed metadata, returns candidate/quarantine only and keeps every extension non-executable.
P0-13 final policy follows stable actual graph, uses unique identities and proves every undeclared/raw edge denied.
P0-14 integrates one service at a time, verifies compatibility/update/rollback and defaults disabled/inventory.
P0-15 runs end-to-end shadow/no-op/observation/quarantine, fault/fuzz/multi-user/security/rollback and absence proof.
Parallel work begins only after frozen prerequisites/interfaces; no local substitute becomes production dependency.
Failure is injected before/after every decode/resolve/policy/proof/audit/auth/dispatch/result/queue/extension/persistence transition.
Kernel, bionic, init, native, HAL/vendor, existing service internals, providers, ROM/OEM, models and public API remain untouched in P0 except approved paths.
Components integrate incrementally in the exact ordered playbook with Android regression between additions.
Global rollback revokes authority/leases, drains, checkpoints, unbinds, rolls back atomically and verifies Android/stale rejection.
G7 evidence hashes source manifest, vectors, API/interfaces, target graph, installed files, policy mapping, tests, signing, Registry/policy and migrations.
The only P0-00 Android-tree delta is documentation/governance/reference evidence; no source, interface, policy, manifest, target or executable.
Pre-existing unrelated checkout changes are preserved, documented and excluded from ProdX attribution.
Requirement IDs are stable; architectural changes require ACP, new version/hash, impact/test/migration/rollback review and re-baseline.
